req.title: Account close
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-014 mandates account closure under two conditions: (1) zero balance closure succeeds, (2) non-zero balance requires sweep or returns 409. Test design applies EP to partition valid/invalid states, BVA to test boundary balances (0, near-0, positive), STC to validate state transitions (open→closed), DT to model balance/sweep decisions, and NEG to verify error pathways. Four tests cover: positive zero-balance closure, negative non-zero without sweep, boundary near-zero, and negative authorization failure.

tests.name: test_close_account_zero_balance_success
tests.executable:
def test_close_account_zero_balance_success():
    acct_id = <fixture_create_account>(balance=0.0)
    auth_token = <fixture_auth_token>
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/{acct_id}/close",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["state"] == "closed"
    assert "final_statement" in data
    assert "closure_timestamp" in data

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_fails
trace: Account state not modified on error

tests.name: test_close_account_boundary_near_zero_balance_succeeds
expected: system behavior at balance > 0 boundary tested
trace: R-FIN-014: Boundary condition at zero threshold
Decision: sweep required for any balance > 0

tests.name: test_close_account_unauthorized_caller_fails
trace: Decision Table: owner mismatch → deny access
